data.description:
# T4 — Import idempotency (skip by source_id) + deferred-push perf + large-import docs

## Context

P1.2 epic rule-swamp-pupil (ab74-b205-72af-4ce1). Hardens the T3 importer: re-runs /
resume-after-crash must never duplicate, and bulk import must not pay a network round-trip
per event. Depends on T3.

## What

- `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` — at import start, one streaming scan of the TARGET store
  builds `{source_id → local_id}`; a line whose `source_id` is already present is SKIPPED
  (never updated — updating existing tickets is sync = reconciler, out of scope).
- Deferred push: import runs with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` for its duration, then a single
  explicit `push.push_tickets_branch(...)` at the end (eliminates per-event pushes).
  Restore prior env state afterward.
- Docs: document the accepted KNOWN LIMITATION (one git commit + one lock cycle per event;
  no batch-commit primitive — deferred to follow-up 39c5-d81a-aad6-4e21) and the guidance to
  pre-compact the source + set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` for large imports
  (`docs/architecture.md` or `docs/concurrency.md`, plus `_cli/help/import.txt`).

## Scope

In: idempotency, deferred-push, docs. Out: a batch-commit primitive (separate follow-up).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Re-running an import (or resuming a killed one) produces ZERO duplicates (skip by
      source_id via a streaming target scan).
- [ ] Existing tickets are never updated by import (skip-only on collision).
- [ ] Import defers push (`REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`) and pushes once at the end; prior env
      restored.
- [ ] Large-import limitation + pre-compact/REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off guidance documented.
- [ ] Tests: idempotent re-run (0 dups), resume-after-partial, deferred-push behavior;
      `pytest` green.
data.title: T4 — Import idempotency + deferred-push perf + docs
